decision.tagline: Two browser deployables, one shared contract spine, zero permission to drift into a generic dashboard.
decision.title: ADR-006 Web Frontend Topology
decision.winner: Shared TypeScript/React web platform with two deployables

deployables.bundle_policy: Keep calm-shell and governance code in one internal deployable with route-level code splitting so internal session and selector grammar stay unified without exposing portal bundles.
deployables.label: Operator Web
deployables.session_posture: Internal authenticated browser surface with receipt-aware mutations, step-up checkpoints, and authority/help handoff returns.
deployables.why_separate: The internal surface shares operator identity posture and can reuse internal shell runtime without shipping customer-safe copy or portal bundles.

deployables.bundle_policy: Portal deployable excludes governance and internal operator modules entirely, and ships only portal-safe selectors, copy, and route families.
deployables.label: Client Portal Web
deployables.session_posture: Customer-safe authenticated browser surface with explicit continuity contracts, plain-language copy, and route-bound upload/approval flows.
deployables.why_separate: Deployable isolation protects customer-safe semantics, bundle size, branding, and blast radius from internal operator/governance changes.

browser_handoff_plan.owner: Playwright covers browser-owned auth/help/handoff surfaces; later native tasks pair this with native automation for desktop-only scenes.
rules: Return to the owning shell and object context after browser handoff.
Preserve focus anchor or explicit fallback target after handoff completion or cancellation.

focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

focus_restore_order: Remap to the closest surviving focus anchor within the same governed object.
Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

Fall back to the owning object summary on the same governed object.
Use the serialized parent return target if the original focus anchor no longer exists.
Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.

Use the narrowest surviving list or queue target with an explicit recovery explanation.
source_refs: Algorithm/frontend_shell_and_interaction_law.md::8. Accessibility, focus, and motion[Reduced-motion must preserve meaning with minimal or no displacement.]
Algorithm/semantic_selector_and_accessibility_contract.md::Shared semantic selector and accessibility contract[Reduced motion cannot change semantic order or action meaning.]

required_assertions: semantic locator presence per shell family
focus return after support panel close
stale/recovery inline message without object loss

priority_order: semantic data-testid only when the selector contract requires a machine anchor
locator_strategy.screenshot_policy: capture golden overviews for topology and shell continuity states

routeGroups.browser_handoff_rule: Authority or identity browser handoff returns to the same manifest object and focus anchor.
routeGroups.continuity_rule: Same manifest or focused workflow object must reopen in the calm shell with the same dominant question and promoted support region policy.
routeGroups.label: Manifest workspace

routeGroups.browser_handoff_rule: Notification-open and refresh preserve the same item or queue anchor; no remount to a different shell family.
routeGroups.continuity_rule: Queue-to-item transitions stay inside the same calm shell and preserve filters, selection, drafts, and module focus when lawful.
routeGroups.label: Operator collaboration

routeGroups.browser_handoff_rule: Browser-owned help, upload, or sign-off checkpoint returns stay bound to the same portal route and object context.
routeGroups.continuity_rule: Home, Documents, Approvals, Onboarding, and Help keep one primary task context and one promoted support region even through refresh or responsive collapse.
routeGroups.label: Portal primary routes

routeGroups.browser_handoff_rule: Return from browser or authority handoff never implies completion until the customer-safe request projection settles.
routeGroups.continuity_rule: Contextual request-detail routes remain in the portal shell and preserve return-path anchors rather than becoming a separate route family.
routeGroups.label: Portal request-context routes

routeGroups.browser_handoff_rule: Step-up, approvals, and audit exports return to the same governance route and selected object context.
routeGroups.continuity_rule: Selected object, filters, and promoted support sidecar stay in the same governance shell across route changes and responsive redock.
routeGroups.label: Governance console

sharedPackages.must_not_hold: framework-specific page composition or branded copy
sharedPackages.responsibility: Generated and hand-authored route, shell, projection, selector, and stale-guard contracts shared by both deployables.
Shell ownership, route grammar, deep-link restore, browser handoff return, and same-object continuity helpers.
Light premium browser token system, shell accents, spacing scales, typography stacks, and motion timing constants.

sharedPackages.must_not_hold: raw API fetching or cache adapters
sharedPackages.responsibility: Operator, portal, and governance interaction-layer bindings mapped from the shell foundation contract.

sharedPackages.must_not_hold: visual-only classes or CSS selectors as test contracts
sharedPackages.responsibility: Semantic selector helpers and accessibility anchor exports for Playwright-first validation.

sharedPackages.must_not_hold: route-local composition or customer-safe copy decisions
sharedPackages.responsibility: Typed API clients, command envelope helpers, receipt polling, upload-session clients, and stream adapters.
Shared cache keys, continuity store utilities, stale/rebase posture state, and session-bound storage guards.
Playwright fixtures, semantic locator helpers, contract-driven mocks, and screenshot harness utilities.

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: What is the current authoritative decision posture for this manifest?
routeVariants.label: Manifest workspace
routeVariants.statusTone: Live bundle aligned with the current manifest seal.

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: What workflow issue attached to this object requires intervention now?
routeVariants.label: Work item workspace
routeVariants.statusTone: Inline stale/rebase posture downgrades unsafe actions without remounting the shell.

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: What is the one next safe task for this client right now?
routeVariants.label: Portal home
routeVariants.statusTone: Customer-safe summary with explicit reassurance and limitation copy.

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: What does the client need to complete this request safely?
routeVariants.label: Request detail
routeVariants.statusTone: Same portal shell, contextual return, no leakage of staff-only semantics.

layoutBudget.compact_mode_rule: redock auxiliary surfaces without changing shell meaning

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: Which governance slice requires review or staged mutation next?
routeVariants.label: Governance overview
routeVariants.statusTone: Dense control-plane workspace with one promoted sidecar and route-stable filters.

routeVariants.dominantQuestion: What evidence explains this configuration or authority change?
routeVariants.label: Audit investigation
routeVariants.statusTone: The same shell remains mounted while the selected object and sidecar context change.

stateDomains.allowed_storage: in-memory query cache only; optional persistent cache only for route-safe replay and never as legal truth
stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not derive new legal posture in the client or persist these projections as durable truth.
stateDomains.purpose: Hydrated route-level read models, receipt follow-up, and stream-backed query state.

stateDomains.allowed_storage: URL, history.state, and tiny session-bound restore records only
stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not hide continuity state behind local component-only memory when the route contract requires cross-refresh restore.
purge_or_invalidation_triggers: browser handoff returns with incompatible route context
stateDomains.purpose: Route ownership, shell stability, focus restoration, return targets, and rebase posture that preserve same-object continuity.

stateDomains.allowed_storage: memory first; session-bound browser storage only when the contract publishes draft-resume semantics
stateDomains.authoritative_owner: deployable-local feature modules with packages/state-runtime guards
stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not silently replay drafts after a stale-view rejection or scope downgrade without explicit user-visible rebasing.
stateDomains.purpose: Unsubmitted route-local drafts, upload selections, approval acknowledgements, and governance change-basket work.

stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not encode business truth, customer-safe visibility, or settlement semantics here.
stateDomains.purpose: Purely presentational state such as drawer openness, filter chips, tab focus, viewport density, and transient highlights.

stateDomains.allowed_storage: httpOnly cookies and server-side session state; no raw tokens in browser-managed JavaScript storage
stateDomains.authoritative_owner: server session boundary plus packages/northbound-clients handoff runtime
stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not collapse operator and portal sessions into one mega-app ambient login state.
stateDomains.purpose: Browser-authenticated session envelopes, anti-CSRF state, and browser handoff return metadata for each deployable.

stateDomains.allowed_storage: session-bound storage or IndexedDB only for transfer/resume metadata that the route contract explicitly allows
stateDomains.forbidden_misuse: Do not store raw authority credentials, legal truth, hidden staff context, or customer-safe widened caches here.
stateDomains.purpose: Route-safe upload-session descriptors, preview/download intents, and limited cached transfer metadata.

verificationLab.focusReturnContract: Closing a support surface returns focus to the invoking control.

scenarios.label: Stale rebase without object loss
scenarios.message: The route keeps the same shell and object anchor, explains the changed basis, and offers rebase instead of remounting elsewhere.

scenarios.label: Deep link restore
scenarios.message: Open directly into the governed object and restore the nearest lawful focus anchor or serialized parent return target.

scenarios.label: Browser back/return continuity
scenarios.message: Back and forward keep the same shell page semantics and do not dissolve the route group into a generic home screen.

scenarios.label: Reduced-motion parity
scenarios.message: Reduced motion removes displacement but preserves hierarchy, state meaning, and disclosure order.
